0.q: DvGPDH1 and DvGPDH2 shared high sequence similarity, with 60.0% nucleotide sequence identity and 62.0% amino acid sequence identity, indicating that the two GPDH genes were homologues.

1.q: Overexpression of DvGPDH1 or DvGPDH2 could complement a yeast GPDH mutant (gpd1Δ)... In vitro assays with purified DvGPDH1 and DvGPDH2 also showed apparent GPDH activity for both.

2.q: Overexpression of DvGPDH1 or DvGPDH2 could complement a yeast GPDH mutant (gpd1Δ)... In vitro assays with purified DvGPDH1 and DvGPDH2 also showed apparent GPDH activity for both.

3.q: overexpression of DvVP indeed partially complemented the G19 mutant phenotype, suppressing salt hypersensitivity ... indicating that DvVP can function as V–H?-PPase in yeast cells.
